patient.phenotype_summary: Male infant with neonatal-onset epileptic encephalopathy: tonic seizures from day 3 of life evolving to epileptic spasms, burst-suppression on EEG, profound global developmental delay. No dysmorphism. Brain MRI reported as normal at 2 months.
patient.family_history: Non-consanguineous parents, no family history of epilepsy or developmental disorders. One healthy older sister.

prior_testing.implication: Large CNVs excluded; small intragenic events and balanced rearrangements still possible — re-examine WGS SV calls below CMA resolution.
Panel covered KCNQ2 coding exons; however v3 did not include deep-intronic or splice-region analysis and reported SNVs/indels only — CNV/SV and non-coding still open.

inheritance_fit.notes: Absent in gnomAD, recurrent de novo gene for this phenotype; parental testing not yet performed.

igv_assessment.observations: Alt allele supported by 20/42 reads, both strands, MAPQ60, no soft-clip enrichment; allele balance 0.48 consistent with heterozygous call.

clinvar.review_status: criteria provided, multiple submitters, no conflicts
clinvar.same_aa_different_nt: p.Ala274 also altered by c.820_821 variants (PM5 candidate)

phenotype_match.notes: Neonatal onset, burst-suppression and epileptic spasms are the textbook KCNQ2-DEE presentation.

literature.title: KCNQ2 encephalopathy: emerging phenotype of a neonatal epileptic encephalopathy
literature.finding: Defines the neonatal DEE phenotype with burst-suppression; pore-region missense variants act by dominant-negative mechanism.

literature.title: Functional characterization of KCNQ2 variants in neonatal epilepsy
literature.finding: Pore-domain substitutions including codon 274 reduce M-current; supports loss/dominant-negative effect (basis for PS3 if assay repeated).

applied_criteria.rationale: Same amino-acid change (p.Ala274Val) reported Pathogenic in ClinVar via an alternate nucleotide change.
Located in the well-established S5-S6 pore mutational hotspot of KCNQ2, depleted of benign variation.
REVEL 0.94, AlphaMissense 0.981, CADD 29.4 — concordant damaging at calibrated moderate strength (Pejaver 2022).
Phenotype-consistent variant in a gene with high de novo rate; de novo status presumed pending parental testing (would upgrade to PS2).

evidence_refs.value: no family history; recurrent de novo gene

considered_not_applied.reason: single proband; trio not yet available — raised as PE001
no patient-specific functional data — see PE002

non_acmg_evidence: Phenotype is highly specific for KCNQ2-DEE (PP4-level specificity).

candidate_variants.evidence_summary: De novo (presumed) pore-domain missense in KCNQ2, absent from population databases, with concordant damaging in-silico predictions and an established ClinVar pathogenic assertion for the identical amino-acid change. Phenotype is the textbook KCNQ2-DEE presentation.

challenges.challenge: Could this be a mapping artifact from a KCNQ2 paralogue/pseudogene?
challenges.rebuttal: MAPQ60 across all reads, UCSC segdup track empty at locus, allele balance 0.48; no paralogous misalignment signature on IGV.

challenges.challenge: De novo status is assumed, not proven; could be inherited from an unaffected mosaic parent, weakening PM6.
challenges.rebuttal: Classification reaches Pathogenic even with PM6 removed (9 pts -> Likely pathogenic). Parental testing requested (PE001) to convert PM6->PS2 and exclude inherited/mosaic origin.
challenges.residual_risk: If maternally inherited, recurrence risk counselling changes; does not alter pathogenicity of the variant itself.

challenges.challenge: Normal MRI argues against some severe DEEs.
challenges.rebuttal: KCNQ2-DEE classically has normal or non-specific early MRI; burst-suppression + neonatal spasms are highly concordant.

challenges.challenge: Was a better candidate missed elsewhere?
challenges.rebuttal: No other variant in the genome reaches LP/P with comparable phenotype fit; this is the singular strong candidate.

adversarial_review.reviewer_verdict: Report as primary diagnosis. The only material open item is proof of de novo origin, which affects counselling more than the pathogenicity call.

overall_assessment.summary: KCNQ2 c.821C>T p.(Ala274Val) is a Pathogenic, phenotype-concordant variant that explains this neonatal epileptic encephalopathy and was missed by the prior panel (likely a reporting/coverage gap). It is the primary diagnostic hypothesis. Confirm de novo status by parental testing for accurate recurrence-risk counselling.

consequence.loftee_flags: PTC in last exon (NMD escape predicted)

inheritance_fit.notes: Single het; SCN2A is a DEE gene but the mechanism for early-onset DEE is typically gain-of-function missense, not LoF.

igv_assessment.observations: Single-base deletion supported by 15/35 reads on both strands; not in a homopolymer run; call looks real.

associated_conditions.name: Developmental and epileptic encephalopathy 11 (early onset, GoF)

missing_cardinal_features: HP:0010851 burst-suppression typical of GoF SCN2A
discordant_features: Onset timing: SCN2A LoF typically causes LATER-onset seizures/autism, not neonatal DEE
phenotype_match.notes: Genotype-mechanism-phenotype mismatch: a LoF variant predicts the later-onset SCN2A phenotype, not this patient's neonatal GoF-type presentation.

literature.title: SCN2A gain- vs loss-of-function genotype-phenotype correlation
literature.finding: Early-onset (<3 mo) DEE is associated with gain-of-function missense; protein-truncating LoF variants associate with later seizures/autism.

applied_criteria.rationale: Frameshift in a LoF-intolerant gene, but located in the last exon with predicted NMD escape -> PVS1 downgraded to Moderate per ClinGen SVI decision tree.
An alternate, fully concordant pathogenic variant (C001 in KCNQ2) explains the phenotype; weak evidence against SCN2A causation.

considered_not_applied.reason: phenotype does NOT match the LoF-SCN2A presentation; deliberately withheld

non_acmg_evidence: Mechanism mismatch (LoF vs required GoF) materially lowers prior probability of causation for THIS phenotype.

candidate_variants.evidence_summary: Rare, real last-exon frameshift in SCN2A. However, neonatal DEE in SCN2A is a gain-of-function disease; a LoF/PTC allele predicts the later-onset autism/seizure phenotype, which this patient does not have. Retained as a VUS and as a secondary finding rather than the diagnosis.

challenges.challenge: LoF SCN2A does not cause neonatal GoF-type DEE; reporting it as causal would be a mechanism error.
challenges.rebuttal: Proposer concedes; variant cannot be the primary cause of the neonatal presentation. Retained as VUS / secondary finding only.
challenges.residual_risk: Could be relevant to long-term autism risk; flagged for re-evaluation as phenotype evolves.

challenges.challenge: Is the LoF mechanism even disease-relevant here?
challenges.rebuttal: SCN2A LoF is disease-relevant but for a different, later-onset phenotype; not applicable to current cardinal features.

adversarial_review.reviewer_verdict: Do not report as the diagnosis. Report as a secondary VUS with explicit mechanism caveat; re-evaluate if an autism/later-seizure phenotype emerges.

overall_assessment.summary: Genuine rare SCN2A frameshift but mechanistically mismatched to the patient's neonatal GoF-type presentation. Kept as a low-confidence VUS / secondary finding, not the diagnosis.

pending_evidence.question: Are both parents wild-type for KCNQ2 c.821C>T (i.e. is the variant de novo)?
pending_evidence.rationale: Confirming de novo origin upgrades PM6 to PS2 and, more importantly, establishes accurate recurrence-risk counselling (de novo ~1% recurrence via gonadal mosaicism vs 50% if a parent is mosaic/affected).

pending_evidence.question: Does the p.Ala274Val substitution reduce KCNQ2/Kv7.2 M-current in a heterologous system?
pending_evidence.rationale: A patient/variant-specific electrophysiology result would supply PS3 and remove any residual doubt about the functional consequence of this specific substitution.

experimental_plan.method: Whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology of KCNQ2 variant in CHO/Xenopus expression system
experimental_plan.objective: Quantify the effect of p.Ala274Val on Kv7.2 (M-type) potassium current amplitude and voltage dependence, including a dominant-negative test by co-expression with wild-type.
experimental_plan.hypothesis: p.Ala274Val reduces M-current density relative to wild-type; co-expression with WT (mimicking the heterozygous state) yields current below the 50% expected for simple haploinsufficiency, indicating a dominant-negative effect.
protocol_outline: Site-directed mutagenesis to introduce c.821C>T into a validated KCNQ2 cDNA construct; Sanger-confirm.
Transfect CHO cells with (a) WT KCNQ2, (b) mutant alone, (c) WT+mutant 1:1, each co-transfected with KCNQ3, plus GFP marker.
48 h post-transfection, whole-cell patch-clamp; voltage step protocol -80 to +40 mV.
Measure current density (pA/pF), V50 of activation, and activation/deactivation kinetics; n>=10 cells/condition across >=3 transfections.
experimental_plan.sample_requirements: No patient sample required (heterologous system); requires validated KCNQ2/KCNQ3 constructs.
controls: a known pathogenic dominant-negative KCNQ2 variant (assay sensitivity control)
a known benign KCNQ2 variant (specificity control)
experimental_plan.readout: M-current density (pA/pF) and biophysical parameters per condition.
experimental_plan.interpretation_framework: PS3 supported if mutant-alone current is <50% of WT AND the WT+mutant condition is significantly below WT (p<0.05) with effect size comparable to the positive dominant-negative control. A current indistinguishable from WT would be evidence AGAINST pathogenicity (potential BS3).
limitations: Heterologous system may not capture neuronal context or auxiliary subunits.
Does not establish in vivo causation, only molecular effect.
Construct/expression artefacts can confound — mitigated by parallel benign/pathogenic controls.

critiques.issue: Without a known dominant-negative positive control, a 'reduced current' result is hard to calibrate against the assay's dynamic range.
critiques.resolution: Positive dominant-negative control already specified in controls; require it to pass before interpreting the test variant.

critiques.issue: M-current is KCNQ2/KCNQ3 heteromeric; testing KCNQ2 alone would under-represent physiology.
critiques.resolution: Protocol co-expresses KCNQ3 in all conditions.

critiques.issue: Result is supportive (PS3) but not decisive given C001 is already Pathogenic on existing evidence — weigh cost/turnaround against clinical value.
critiques.resolution: Down-prioritised to medium; PE001 (segregation) is the higher-value action.

pending_evidence.question: Why did the v3 epilepsy panel not report KCNQ2 c.821C>T despite covering the gene?
pending_evidence.rationale: Clarifying whether this was a coverage gap, a filtering threshold, or a classification difference informs lab QA and confirms the WGS finding is not a false positive unique to this pipeline.

excluded_variants_summary.reason: gnomAD AF 0.31 (BA1) — common polymorphism, not disease-causing.
Pathogenic for cystic fibrosis but no phenotypic overlap with neonatal epileptic encephalopathy; incidental carrier finding.
10 kb deletion call failed IGV review: both breakpoints in segmental duplication, supported by soft-clipped reads only, no discordant pairs — technical artifact (unrefuted fatal challenge).

run_summary.headline: A Pathogenic de novo (presumed) KCNQ2 pore-domain variant explains this neonatal epileptic encephalopathy and was missed by the prior gene panel. One secondary VUS (SCN2A) is retained with a mechanism caveat.
